items.summary: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

items.title: Iran will not boycott FIFA World Cup, but seeks to get United States-based matches moved
items.summary: Iran has no intention of boycotting the FIFA World Cup, but is seeking to have its group stage matches moved out of the United States.

items.title: Bundaberg residents call for buyback scheme after three major floods in 16 years
items.summary: There are calls for the Queensland government to buy back homes in Bundaberg's flood zones, but the disaster recovery minister says the focus is on mitigation measures like floodgates.

items.title: Unemployment rate rises to 4.3 per cent, but rate hike fears persist amid inflation concerns
items.summary: Australia's unemployment rate rose to 4.3 per cent in February, up from 4.1 per cent, in seasonally adjusted terms.

items.title: Israel hits Iran South Pars facilities in world's largest natural gas field
items.summary: The Israeli bombing of Iranian facilities in the South Pars gas field and Iran's retaliation targeting energy infrastructure across the Persian Gulf, mark a significant point of escalation in the US-Israeli war with Iran.

items.title: Prolonged Middle East war and AI profitability 'downgrades' are major risks for economy, RBA says
items.summary: Interest rates, war in the Middle East and AI advancements are expected to hit the Australian economy, but the RBA says we are prepared.

items.title: Albanese unveils new national fuel supply taskforce to steer response to Middle East war
items.summary: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese has named Anthea Harris, a former chief of Australia's energy regulator, as the national coordinator of the taskforce.

items.title: Botanist gave early warning on rehab difficulties for troubled Griffin Coal's Collie mine site
items.summary: The WA government insists Griffin Coal's obligations have not changed, but changes to its mining agreement could allow the company to walk away from rehabilitating its Collie mine site.

items.title: Iranian oil tankers are using fake data to get sanctioned fuel out of the Strait of Hormuz
items.summary: Changing flags, creating new names, and faking their GPS data, ABC NEWS Verify investigates how Iranian-linked oil tankers continue to export oil during the war.

items.title: Ship crew involved in French Polynesia cocaine bust sent to immigration detention
items.summary: The crew of a vessel that was intercepted carrying nearly 5 tonnes of cocaine has been escorted off the ship by Australian authorities.

items.title: Australia isn't sending a warship to the Persian Gulf. But if the government wanted to, is there one to send?
items.summary: Australia has made it clear it's not planning to send a warship to the Strait of Hormuz and naval experts warn our ships would be of minimal help if they were deployed.

items.title: Cable Beach venue in Western Australia's far north charged with liquor breaches
items.summary: A cafe in the Kimberley town that has strict alcohol restrictions has been charged with multiple liquor breaches, while two of its workers have been fined.

items.title: ACCC investigating allegations of anti-competitive conduct by major fuel suppliers
items.summary: The competition watchdog has announced it is investigating allegations of anti-competitive conduct by major fuel suppliers in regional and rural Australia.

items.title: NRL news: Dragons forward Loko Pasifiki Tonga cleared of serious injury after being hospitalised
items.summary: A Dragons training session ends with a player stretchered off and assisted from the ground into an ambulance before being rushed to hospital.

items.title: Dance performance takes on expectations of disability, demanding the right to take risks
items.summary: Challenging assumptions about what disabled people can and can't do is at the centre of this performance at the Joondalup Festival.

items.title: AFL news wrap: Matt Rowell set to see physio on broken finger, Hawks prepare to reach 'top notch' against Swans
items.summary: Gold Coast has started its 2026 campaign well, even while last year's Brownlow medallist continues to be sidelined due to a broken finger.

items.title: Why did the RBA raise interest rates? Will higher petrol prices have same effect?
items.summary: This week's rate rise left ABC readers asking "how would hiking rates stop inflation caused by an unavoidable cost like petrol that's spiking due to a war on the other side of the world?"

items.title: Galatasaray player Noa Lang suffers freak hand injury, Barcelona knocks out Newcastle out of UEFA Champions League
items.summary: Galatasaray forward Noa Lang suffered a freak hand injury when he caught his thumb on an advertising hoarding in his team's 4-0 loss to Liverpool in the Champions League.

items.title: BOM declares cyclone watch for NT communities with Tropical Cyclone Narelle set to hit on Saturday
items.summary: Parts of the Northern Territory have been issued a cyclone watch with Severe Tropical Cyclone Narelle expected to make landfall on Groote Eylandt from Saturday as a category three system.

items.title: Senegal calls for corruption investigation after Africa Cup of Nations Nations title stripped
items.summary: The Senegalese Football Federation plans to appeal the decision that gave its Africa Cup of Nations title to Morocco, with Senegal's government calling for an investigation into alleged corruption of African football's governing body.

items.title: Police investigate deaths of kangaroos and joey killed in Lake Macquarie hit-and-run
items.summary: Police are investigating the deaths of kangaroos on suburban streets in Lake Macquarie in what has been described as a "deliberate" hit-and-run attack.

items.title: Severe Tropical Cyclone Narelle to cross Qld coast as category five system
items.summary: Severe Tropical Cyclone Narelle is expected to be a category five system when it crosses the coast, producing winds near its eye of 315 kilometres per hour.

items.title: Wind gusts reach 315km/h as category five Cyclone Narelle nears Qld coast — as it happened
items.summary: Severe Tropical Cyclone Narelle has intensified into a category five system and is expected to cross the coast on Friday morning. Read back on how events on Thursday unfolded.

items.title: Cost-of-living crisis widens regional-metro higher education gap
items.summary: Pursuing higher education is difficult for regional students who uproot their lives, with advocates calling for support to level the playing field.

items.title: The secret bush sport that's been hidden away for three decades

items.title: Cyclone Narelle tracker map: Where is it forecast to cross Queensland?
items.summary: Track the path of Tropical Cyclone Narelle and see live wind speeds as the system crosses Far North Queensland.

items.title: Brisbane's 'third places' providing a spot away from work, home, and online echo chambers
items.summary: In an increasingly digital world, Brisbane residents are still heading out to enjoy third places to take a break, enjoy life, and expose themselves to new ideas.

items.title: No 'definitive time frame' to end Iran war, Pentagon to seek funds from Congress — as it happened
items.summary: US Secretary of Defense Pete Hegseth says the US will end the war in Iran quickly, because the US President Donald Trump "knows better" than to keep it going for a long time.

items.title: Can ARN Media financially survive a legal battle with Kyle Sandilands and Jackie 'O' Henderson?
items.summary: Even prior to this month's colossal blow-up of the Kyle and Jackie O Show, ARN Media's future had already been teetering on the edge.

items.title: Checking online for fuel prices? Here's what to keep in mind
items.summary: Petrol Spy, a national website that displays petrol, diesel and LPG prices across the country, says page views have gone up six to eight times since the Iran conflict started.

items.title: Koala habitat logged by farmers caught unaware of new environment laws
items.summary: Koala habitat has been bulldozed in an apparent breach of new environment laws that took effect in December, with fears farmers are unaware of their new obligations.

items.title: Women's Asian Cup 2026: Matildas on notice as Japan soar into final — here's what we learnt from the second semifinal
items.summary: Ultimate control, goals from everywhere and an eccentric coach from Greenland. This is what we learned from Japan's supremely impressive semifinal victory over South Korea.

items.title: Some South Australians will vote in two elections this weekend
items.summary: Thousands of First Nations South Australians will be eligible to vote in two elections on Saturday, in a potential historic first for the state.

items.title: Could a fracture in conservative politics cost right-wing parties a seat in SA's parliament?
items.summary: Labor's popularity in South Australia is set to lead to a large number of seats for the party at the upcoming state election, as various right-wing parties compete for a red seat in the upper house, writes ABC Chief Elections and Data Analyst Casey Briggs.

items.title: Price gouging at petrol stations may not be illegal, experts warn, as PM considers supply chain tsar
items.summary: Drivers fed up with massive price spikes at the bowser are being warned not to expect a wave of fuel company prosecutions over allegations of price gouging.

items.title: Why don't Australian banks offer 30-year fixed-rate home loans? Borrowers would benefit from them
items.summary: A new report says Australian borrowers would benefit from 30-year fixed-rate home loans, and they should be made available.

items.title: Steve Irwin made totemic crocodile Old Faithful famous. Recently laws may have been broken in its capture
items.summary: Missing: One 80-year-old, 4.5 metre totemic crocodile. Last known whereabouts: Australia Zoo. Welcome to the fight for Old Faithful.

items.title: Pressure mounts on government to release cabinet documents sooner after motion passes parliament
items.summary: Tasmania's minority parliament has delivered the Liberal government a stinging verdict on integrity and transparency, with two motions passed without the government's support.

items.title: Ryan Gosling is humanity's last hope in the new space epic Project Hail Mary
items.summary: Project Hail Mary hits Australian cinemas this week, joining Oscar-nominated animation Arco, off-the-wall slasher Dolly and BAFTA-winning biopic I Swear.

items.title: Put your Oscars knowledge to the test with our 2026 Academy Awards quiz
items.summary: Awards season might be over for another year but the memories will live on in our minds … and in the questions of this Academy Awards quiz.

items.title: Heart surgeries in Tasmania reduced from daily to two a week amid indefinite industrial action
items.summary: Daily heart surgeries have been cut to two a week in Tasmania while a small group of crucial specialists strikes for better pay and conditions. But negotiations with the government have halted, and locums are being sought to plug resulting healthcare gaps.

items.title: Treasury can't predict how Trump ends this war, with or without allies
items.summary: Even the best minds at Treasury can't know what will happen with global oil supplies, or how Donald Trump plans to end this war, with or without the help of allies.

items.title: Iranian women's football team back in Iran, state media announces
items.summary: The delegation of players and staff included five people who initially accepted Australia's offer of asylum but later changed their minds.

items.title: Military officers arrested over acid attack on human rights campaigner Andrie Yunus in Jakarta
items.summary: Four Indonesian officers have been arrested and detained over an acid attack on human rights campaigner Andrie Yunus in Jakarta.

items.title: Australia to toughen tobacco crime laws as cigarette and vape black markets surge
items.summary: Criminal gangs cashing in on Australia's illicit tobacco boom will face tougher penalties and expanded police surveillance powers in a fresh crackdown on the black market.

items.title: What is a healthy birth weight? Urgency to close the gap for all babies
items.summary: The Productivity Commission's latest Closing the Gap data shows just four out of 19 targets are on track. Advocates call for urgent funding to address low birth weights.

items.title: ACT Brumbies veteran James Slipper set to break Super Rugby's record for most games played
items.summary: The prop will have played more Super Rugby than anyone else and is hoping to continue next season.

items.title: Japan storm into Asian Cup final against Matildas with ominous dismantling of South Korea
items.summary: Japan has produced a frighteningly good display to overpower South Korea 4-1, booking a spot in the Women's Asian Cup final against the Matildas.

items.title: Wednesday finance with Daniel Ziffer
items.summary: News from the world of finance and business. Top stories in text, video and audio. Business video news bulletin. Features and analysis.

items.title: Pauline Hanson in confrontations with SA premier and MP as One Nation poll support continues
items.summary: South Australia's premier tells Pauline Hanson he will work with any One Nation MPs elected on Saturday, as a new poll shows the right-wing party remains ahead of the Liberals.

items.title: Possum found in Hobart Airport gift shop soft toy section
items.summary: A real possum nestled among soft toys and animals in a gift shop has thrilled passengers — but caused "pandemonium" after going on a sightseeing tour of the departure lounge.

items.title: Call for tougher anti-vilification penalties as hate flyers target Perth LGBTQIA+ community
items.summary: Police are investigating flyers dropped in mailboxes across Perth targeting members of the LGBTQIA+ community.

items.title: Hawks warned not to underestimate Swans as AFL ladder leaders lose Isaac Heeney to injury
items.summary: The Sydney Swans will be understrength after losing another key player to injury ahead of facing Hawthorn at the MCG tomorrow night.

items.title: Daly River evacuee's heartbreak at seeing community flooded for second time this year
items.summary: Flood evacuees from Daly River in the remote Northern Territory — who have now spent more than a month this year in evacuation centres — say many had lost "everything they worked for".

items.title: Donald Trump, Xi Jinping and the meeting looming over Middle East war
items.summary: The US president said last month that his planned visit to China on March 31 would be "a wild one". A lot has changed since that confident declaration and most of it has weakened his position.

items.title: Bulldogs challenge Bronson Xerri to make case for NRL return, Dophins ready for Sharks fans' abuse
items.summary: Cameron Ciraldo speaks for the first time since Bronson Xerri's discontent at the Bulldogs emerged over the weekend.

items.title: Cape York residents urged to make a plan to shelter from Cyclone Narelle
items.summary: There are "places of refuge" but no designated cyclone shelters on Cape York Peninsula. Local leaders say residents need to make a plan to stay safe as a potential category four or five storm approaches the Queensland coast.

items.title: NSW Police investigating DJ Haram for potential racial hate law breaches at Biennale of Sydney
items.summary: In a video uploaded to social media, Zubeyda Muzeyyen, known as DJ Haram, can be heard urging attendees to "oppose the Zio-Australian-Epstein empire" before she led the crowd with a chant.

items.title: Labour Hire Authority links worker exploitation in the Yarra Valley to organised crime
items.summary: The Labour Hire Authority has accused an apple grower and labour-hire business of exploiting workers in the Yarra Valley, as concerns emerge about organised crime links.

items.title: Narelle is expected to become a category five cyclone — what does that mean?
items.summary: Cyclone Narelle is expected to intensify into a category five cyclone — find out what that means.

items.title: Liberals defend SA election flyer that confused Palestinian flag with another
items.summary: The South Australian Liberals have defended an election campaign flyer that attacks a Labor MP for his views on the Middle East and carries the slogan "Defend Australian Values", but appears to mistakenly show an image of a Sudanese flag instead of a Palestinian one.

items.title: Oil tankers have never stopped, industry assures, though they are in uncharted waters
items.summary: Ships carrying oil are continuing to come to Australia as expected and should do so for the next month, and the industry is urging that while the picture beyond that is uncertain and complicated, "the tankers have never stopped coming".

items.title: Australian facilities damaged in Iranian attack on Middle East air base
items.summary: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese has revealed that an Iranian projectile hit a road just outside the Al Minhad Air Base, but no Australians were injured.

items.title: Is antibacterial or regular hand soap best for killing germs?
items.summary: Experts say there is an array of options when it comes to hand soaps, and some labels can be misleading.

items.title: Fuel costs from Iran war forcing Australians to cancel domestic road travel, flights, cruises
items.summary: Domestic travel is also taking a hit as a result of rising fuel costs across the globe, with regional communities likely to feel the most impact.

items.title: Aboriginal rangers meet on NSW South Coast to merge past and present
items.summary: The first Aboriginal rangers conference for the NSW South Coast focused on how to best take care of country by combining cultural knowledge from elders with new technology.

items.title: John Travolta's old luxury jet bound for Australian aviation museum by ship
items.summary: The long-awaited fuselage and wings of John Travolta's Boeing 707, which he is donating to a regional NSW aircraft museum, have been loaded onto a ship in the US, bound for Australia.
